completed_tasks.name: Fix subagent spans sharing parent trace_id (process inside workflow span)
Add span_name_prefix to distinguish sub-agent turns from parent turns
Remove turn.number attribute and turn_count from SessionState/hook
trace_viewer: word wrap, position memory, OKAHU title, tree lines, span_id on highlight
trace_viewer: fix JSON loading for truncated/partial files
Fix silent ss.turn_count crash preventing workflow span export

remaining_tasks.name: CRITICAL REGRESSION: removing turn_count broke state save, causing offset to never update — hook re-processes ALL accumulated turns every invocation, producing 100+ span traces. State file has stale offset. Next hook run will do one big catch-up, then normalize. MUST verify workflow span appears in next trace.
Capture mid-turn queue-operation messages in spans

blockers.description: Removing turn_count from SessionState caused silent AttributeError in state save dict (line 300 had ss.turn_count). Fixed now, but stale offset in monocle_state.json means next hook invocation will replay all accumulated turns as one big trace. After that catch-up, traces should normalize.

decisions.decision: Subagent spans emitted inside workflow span's OTel context
decisions.rationale: Ensures subagent spans share parent trace_id instead of getting orphaned with random trace_id
No other Monocle framework uses it; caused confusing numbering across hook invocations

decisions.decision: Span names: 'Claude Code' for parent, 'Sub-Agent: {type}' for subagents
decisions.rationale: Clear visual distinction without turn numbers

decisions.decision: trace_viewer live-reloads modified files via mtime tracking
decisions.rationale: File exporter appends spans across hook invocations; viewer must pick up new spans

context_notes: The turn_count removal had a missed reference at monocle_hook.py:300 that caused silent crashes for every hook invocation since. All recent traces (12:28-12:47) are broken (no workflow span, 100+ spans from replayed turns). The fix is in place but needs verification on next hook run. User wants either: keep turn_count internally but don't save to monocle JSON, OR find another way to avoid the offset regression.
